description: Mutex added to Counter to protect concurrent increment from data race
response: [{"id":"H1","hunk_id":"hunk_f0_h0","question":"Under what condition would the original Counter.Inc() produce incorrect results despite appearing to work in tests?","options":["When Inc() is called more than math.MaxInt times, causing integer overflow","When two goroutines call Inc() concurrently, causing a data race on the value field","When the Counter is allocated on the heap rather than the stack","When Inc() is called before the Counter struct is initialized"],"answer":1,"hint":"Integer increment in Go compiles to a read-modify-write sequence — what happens when the CPU scheduler interleaves two goroutines at that boundary?","explanation":"Go's ++ operator is not atomic: it reads value, adds 1, and writes back. If two goroutines both read the same value before either writes back, one increment is lost. Single-goroutine and low-concurrency tests rarely expose this because the race window is narrow — but it is a real data race detectable with go test -race."}]
